critic_raw:
Assessor A: AUROC is appropriate for model selection but insufficient for deployment authorization of an alerting system. The specific operational constraint (2 alerts per 30-bed unit per shift) requires reporting PPV and alert rate at the corresponding threshold. These are not in the paper. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor B: The three missing metrics — PPV at threshold, alert rate, actionable-alert fraction — are all computable from the retrospective dataset. The paper chose to report only AUROC and sensitivity-specificity curves averaged over all thresholds. For a clinical deployment decision, threshold-averaged metrics are insufficient. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor C: Alarm fatigue is specifically called out by nursing leadership as a risk. The paper's recommendation to move toward deployment without addressing this concern is premature. The data to address it is available. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Synthesis: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed. ETD: threshold-specific operational metrics from existing retrospective data.

empirical_test.measure: PPV and alert rate per patient-day at the nursing-capacity-constrained threshold
empirical_test.success_criterion: PPV is clinically meaningful and alert rate within capacity on retrospective data
empirical_test.failure_criterion: PPV at constrained threshold is below clinical utility or alert rate exceeds capacity
